---
title: "Spectral Analysis of Turbulence"
subtitle: "Energy spectra from DNS data"
author: "Your Name"
date: "2026-05-20"
description: "Computing and analysing energy spectra from direct numerical simulation data using MATLAB."
---

## Introduction

In turbulent flows, the energy spectrum $E(k)$ describes how kinetic energy is distributed 
across different length scales. For homogeneous isotropic turbulence, Kolmogorov's theory 
predicts a $k^{-5/3}$ scaling in the inertial subrange:

$$
E(k) = C_K \varepsilon^{2/3} k^{-5/3}
$$

where $C_K \approx 1.5$ is the Kolmogorov constant, $\varepsilon$ is the dissipation rate, 
and $k$ is the wavenumber.

## MATLAB Implementation

::: {.callout-note}
MATLAB code is shown with syntax highlighting. Figures are pre-rendered from MATLAB 
and included as images. The full scripts are available in the 
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/your-username/spectral-tools).
:::

### Loading and preprocessing DNS data

```matlab
%% Load DNS velocity field
% Data from a 256^3 simulation at Re_lambda = 100
load('dns_velocity_field.mat', 'u', 'v', 'w', 'Lx', 'Ly', 'Lz')

[Nx, Ny, Nz] = size(u);
dx = Lx / Nx;

fprintf('Grid: %d x %d x %d\n', Nx, Ny, Nz)
fprintf('Domain: %.2f x %.2f x %.2f\n', Lx, Ly, Lz)
fprintf('Resolution: dx = %.4f\n', dx)
```

### Computing the 3D energy spectrum

```matlab
%% Compute 3D energy spectrum using FFT
% Fourier transform of velocity components
u_hat = fftn(u) / (Nx * Ny * Nz);
v_hat = fftn(v) / (Nx * Ny * Nz);
w_hat = fftn(w) / (Nx * Ny * Nz);

% Energy in Fourier space
E_hat = 0.5 * (abs(u_hat).^2 + abs(v_hat).^2 + abs(w_hat).^2);

% Wavenumber grid
kx = [0:Nx/2-1, -Nx/2:-1] * (2*pi/Lx);
ky = [0:Ny/2-1, -Ny/2:-1] * (2*pi/Ly);
kz = [0:Nz/2-1, -Nz/2:-1] * (2*pi/Lz);
[KX, KY, KZ] = meshgrid(kx, ky, kz);
K_mag = sqrt(KX.^2 + KY.^2 + KZ.^2);

% Shell-averaged spectrum
k_max = floor(Nx/2);
k_shells = 1:k_max;
E_k = zeros(size(k_shells));

for i = 1:length(k_shells)
    shell = (K_mag >= k_shells(i) - 0.5) & (K_mag < k_shells(i) + 0.5);
    E_k(i) = sum(E_hat(shell), 'all');
end
```

### Result

The computed energy spectrum shows excellent agreement with the $k^{-5/3}$ scaling 
in the inertial subrange:

<!-- Pre-rendered figure from MATLAB -->
<!-- Replace with your actual figure: -->
<!-- ![Energy spectrum](images/energy_spectrum.png){#fig-spectrum fig-alt="Energy spectrum"} -->

::: {.callout-tip}
## Placeholder
Replace the image path above with your actual MATLAB figure. 
Export from MATLAB with:
```matlab
exportgraphics(gcf, 'images/energy_spectrum.png', 'Resolution', 300)
```
:::

### Compensated spectrum

To better identify the inertial range, we plot the compensated spectrum 
$E(k) \cdot k^{5/3} \cdot \varepsilon^{-2/3}$:

```matlab
%% Compensated spectrum
epsilon = 2 * nu * sum(k_shells.^2 .* E_k);  % dissipation rate
E_compensated = E_k .* k_shells.^(5/3) .* epsilon^(-2/3);

figure('Position', [100 100 600 400])
semilogx(k_shells, E_compensated, 'b-', 'LineWidth', 1.5)
hold on
yline(1.5, 'r--', 'C_K = 1.5', 'LineWidth', 1.2)
xlabel('k')
ylabel('E(k) k^{5/3} \epsilon^{-2/3}')
title('Compensated Energy Spectrum')
grid on
set(gca, 'FontSize', 12)
exportgraphics(gcf, 'images/compensated_spectrum.png', 'Resolution', 300)
```

<!-- ![Compensated spectrum](images/compensated_spectrum.png){#fig-compensated} -->


## Key findings

The analysis confirms:

1. The $k^{-5/3}$ scaling spans approximately one decade in wavenumber space ($10 \leq k \leq 100$)
2. The Kolmogorov constant $C_K \approx 1.52$, consistent with literature values
3. The dissipation range onset occurs at $k\eta \approx 0.1$, where $\eta$ is the Kolmogorov length scale


## References

- Pope, S. B. (2000). *Turbulent Flows*. Cambridge University Press.
- Ishihara, T., Gotoh, T., & Kaneda, Y. (2009). Study of high-Reynolds number 
  isotropic turbulence by direct numerical simulation. *Annual Review of Fluid Mechanics*, 
  41, 165–180.
